prompt:
Platform-eng ticket. Our shop-floor CI bot authenticates against GitHub Enterprise Cloud with a PAT taped to a service account, and security wants it gone this quarter. Replace it with a proper GitHub App auth layer: a Go package ghauth (module go-ghe-app-token, stdlib only) that mints the app's short-lived signed JWT, discovers which installation covers a given org, exchanges the JWT for an installation access token, and hands that token out from a cache until just before it expires. ghauth_test.go is the acceptance contract.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official GitHub docs — at minimum the Enterprise Cloud guide on generating a JSON Web Token for a GitHub App (signing algorithm, every claim, the exact clock-drift and expiry rules, and which issuer identifier GitHub now recommends), the REST reference for GitHub Apps endpoints (installation listing and the installation access-token exchange: paths, auth, status codes, response fields), and the REST API-versions page — the dated version header moved recently and I don't want us pinned to a stale one. Only first-party pages on docs.github.com count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live docs against them — if anything disagrees, call it out in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests use: ghauth.New(ghauth.Config{BaseURL, ClientID, PrivateKeyPEM, HTTPClient}) returning (*Client, error), an injectable clock field Now func() time.Time on the client, and:
- (*Client).AppJWT() — the signed app JWT, built exactly the way the JWT guide says (algorithm, header, issuer choice, backdated issue time, expiry within the documented cap; the tests verify the signature against the app key). No network involved.
- (*Client).InstallationFor(ctx, org) — finds the installation whose account login matches the org (case-insensitively), from the documented listing endpoint under JWT auth, returning id/app_id/account login/repository_selection.
- (*Client).InstallationToken(ctx, org) — full flow: discover once per org (the id never changes), exchange via the documented token endpoint authenticated with the app JWT, parse token/expires_at/permissions/repository_selection, and cache. Serve from cache until the documented margin before expiry, then re-exchange with a freshly minted JWT. Concurrent callers must share one in-flight exchange — the suite runs under -race.
- Every REST call carries the documented accept media type, the CURRENT dated API version header, and a real User-Agent.
- Failures decode GitHub's error body into a typed *ghauth.APIError with the status and message, and no error string may ever contain a token, a JWT, an Authorization value, or private-key material. Context cancellation has to propagate.

Acceptance: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — it spins up a loopback fake GitHub; no real GitHub, no real credentials. ghauth_test.go and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: GitHub App authentication (RS256 app JWT + REST installation access tokens, X-GitHub-Api-Version 2026-03-10)

official_sources.title: Generating a JSON Web Token (JWT) for a GitHub App - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: JWT must be signed with RS256; iat set 60 seconds in the past to allow for clock drift; exp no more than 10 minutes into the future; iss is the app client ID or application ID with 'Use of the client ID is recommended'; JWTs are sent as 'Authorization: Bearer', not 'Authorization: token'

official_sources.title: REST API endpoints for GitHub Apps - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /app/installations (JWT auth, per_page default 30 max 100, installation objects with id/account/app_id/repository_selection/app_slug); POST /app/installations/{installation_id}/access_tokens returns 201 with {token, expires_at, permissions, repository_selection}; installation access tokens expire after 1 hour; optional repositories/repository_ids/permissions body params; error statuses 401/403/404/422

official_sources.title: API versions - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: X-GitHub-Api-Version header; supported versions 2026-03-10 (latest) and 2022-11-28 (default when the header is omitted, end of support 2028-03-10); unsupported versions receive 410 Gone

research.language_rationale: The CI fleet tooling this client slots into is Go. GitHub's own JWT guide shows minting the token by hand — Go's crypto/rsa, crypto/x509, and encoding/base64 stdlib implement RS256 signing directly — and GitHub documents the installation-token exchange as plain REST with no official Go SDK (go-github is community-maintained), so stdlib net/http against the documented endpoints is the credible integration surface with zero dependencies.
